PICKERSGILL RETIREMENT COMMUNITY

CCN: 215259 · TOWSON, MD 21204 · Baltimore County

ℹ CCRC

Overview

Address
615 CHESTNUT AVENUE, TOWSON, MD 21204
Phone
4108257423
Certified beds
43
Avg daily residents
33 (76% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1994-02-01
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-01-31 12 12 0 64 1 64
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2020-02-28 4 4 0 16 1 16
Total weighted health score
52.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (19)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

19 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0947 Ensure nurse aides have the skills they need to care for residents, and give nurse aides education in dementia care and abuse prevention. D 2025-01-31 2025-03-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2020-02-28 2020-03-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2020-02-28 2020-03-16
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2020-02-28 2020-03-16
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2020-02-28 2020-03-16
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2018-08-16 2018-09-06
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2018-08-16 2018-09-06
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2018-08-16 2018-09-06
